_risk_level_doc: Single constant change in one agent-prompt file. No infrastructure, no schema, no API surface affected.

requirement.original: Fix: Push-analyst grant TTL too short (180s) causes expired grants. agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 sets PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180. After dispatching push-analyst and waiting for it to complete, the orchestrator then processes the result and calls execute-push.py. This round-trip frequently exceeds 180 seconds, causing the grant to be expired when execute-push.py reads it. Increase PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS to 600 (10 minutes).
requirement.clarified: Change PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS from 180 to 600 in agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 Python snippet, and update the adjacent TTL comment to reflect the new value and round-trip latency rationale.
requirement.what: Increase TTL constant from 180 to 600 in one location
requirement.why: Orchestrator round-trip (dispatch -> push-analyst completion -> result processing -> execute-push.py) frequently exceeds 180s, causing grant expiry before consumption

included: agents/push-analyst.md: TTL constant and adjacent comment only
hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh: lines 64, 73, 86 — assertions that check value == 180 must be updated to 600
commands/push.md: lines 216-228 — push-analyst grant TTL prose section references 180s; must be updated to 600s

success_criteria: agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 Python snippet reads PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600

Diff touches exactly agents/push-analyst.md, hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh, and commands/push.md; within agents/push-analyst.md only the TTL assignment and immediately adjacent TTL explanatory comment are modified
constraints: Value must be exactly 600; changes are limited to agents/push-analyst.md (TTL constant and adjacent comment only), hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh (AC4 V1/V2/V3 assertions at lines 64, 73, 86 only), and commands/push.md (Push-analyst grant TTL section lines 216-228 only)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: execute-push.py reads an expired push-analyst grant and aborts the push
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 is too short for the orchestrator -> push-analyst -> orchestrator result-processing -> execute-push.py round trip
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — TTL value set by prior task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: TTL was previously increased from 120s to 180s to absorb subagent dispatch latency; that increment proved insufficient for slow/cold paths
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Grant expires before execute-push.py can validate and consume it, causing push abort
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete for MICRO tier — constant value and location confirmed by direct file read
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Read agents/push-analyst.md lines 158-165; constant is on line 164 inside the Phase 7 Python snippet block

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: MICRO tier — constant location confirmed by direct read; bisect N/A

requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: comment updated to reflect 600s and current round-trip rationale

requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Lines 64, 73, and 86 of hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh updated: all occurrences of 180 in AC4 assertions replaced with 600 so the suite passes after the constant change

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: commands/push.md documents the push-analyst grant TTL
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Lines 216-228 of commands/push.md updated: stale references to PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 and '180 seconds' / '180s' replaced with 600 and the correct round-trip rationale

component_chain.role: Phase 7 grant token writer; computes expires_at using PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS
component_chain.evidence: Read lines 158-165 directly; constant is at line 164

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: After dispatching push-analyst and waiting for it to complete, the orchestrator then processes the result and calls execute-push.py. This round-trip frequently exceeds 180 seconds, causing the grant to be expired when execute-push.py reads it.

expected.source: User requirement verbatim — exact value specified by user

evidence.gap: Current TTL 180s is 420s shorter than required 600s

all_occurrences: hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:64 — grep assertion checking value == 180 (AC4 V1)
hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:73 — Python AST assertion val.value == 180 (AC4 V2)
hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:86 — grep assertion '180 s|seconds?|TTL.*180' in commands/push.md (AC4 V3)
commands/push.md:225 — prose: 'The 180s TTL is the named constant'

reference_source.description: User-supplied verbatim requirement specifying target value 600 and rationale

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts for this specific task ID

context.codebase_state: master branch, agents/push-analyst.md exists at expected path
context.recent_commits: See git log; prior TTL change was task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4

development_approach.strategy: Locate PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 by content match in agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 Python snippet; change value to 600; update adjacent comment on lines 159-160 to describe the 600s rationale and cite task dev-20260527-063758-T3.
development_approach.validation_approach: Grep agents/push-analyst.md for PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600 (should appear). Grep hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh for 180 (should NOT appear on lines 64, 73, 86 — all three assertions must reference 600). Grep commands/push.md for '180' in lines 216-228 (should NOT appear — prose must reference 600s). Verify diff touches exactly these three files and no others.

research_findings.reason: MICRO tier straightforward constant change with user-specified value; no research warranted

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified five findings: (1) comment task-cite is expansionist if task ID unknown; (2) comment wording should cite specific round-trip path; (3) AC1 line-number assertion is brittle; (4) AC2 should constrain the whole diff; (5) security note wording should be more precise.

findings.issue: Instructing dev to 'cite the current task' in comment is expansionist if task ID is not provided to dev
findings.proposed_fix: Intended change: Change PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS from 180 to 600, and update only the adjacent TTL comment to state the new 600s/10-minute rationale.
findings.rationale: Task ID dev-20260527-063758-T3 is known and included in comment wording suggestion; the concern is addressed by supplying the value explicitly in Technical Hints

findings.issue: Comment update should reflect user-stated root cause (round-trip latency), not just generic 'subagent dispatch latency'
findings.proposed_fix: Comment wording: 'The TTL is 600s to cover the orchestrator -> push-analyst -> orchestrator result-processing -> execute-push.py round trip, which frequently exceeded the previous 180s window.'
findings.rationale: Adopted verbatim in Technical Hints suggested comment wording

findings.issue: AC1 asserting absolute line 164 is brittle if comment editing shifts line numbers
findings.proposed_fix: AC1: GIVEN agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7, WHEN change applied, THEN Python snippet assigns PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600
findings.rationale: AC1 rewritten to assert content pattern rather than absolute line number

findings.issue: AC2 should constrain the whole diff, not just within-file scope
findings.proposed_fix: AC2: The entire diff is limited to agents/push-analyst.md; within that file, only the TTL assignment and immediately adjacent TTL explanatory comment are modified.
findings.rationale: AC2 rewritten to explicitly state diff is limited to one file

findings.issue: Security note overstates 'does not weaken security'; longer TTL does increase validity window
findings.proposed_fix: Security note: This deliberately increases the bounded validity window to 600s; existing single-use consumption and nonce/request/head/branch/remote validation behavior remains unchanged.
findings.rationale: More accurate wording adopted in Edge Cases & Risks section

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All five codex findings applied: AC1 rewritten to content-match assertion; AC2 scopes full diff; comment wording updated to cite round-trip path; security note made more precise; task ID supplied explicitly rather than asking dev to invent it.
